destinations.samplePrompt: Plan a 3-day Switzerland trip with scenic trains, a reliable mountain route, a quiet cafe, and one local warning.

cards.title: SWISS flights into Zurich
cards.summary: Good base option for a Switzerland trip because Zurich Airport has the easiest rail connections into the Alps.
cards.trustSummary: Based on public carrier pages and airport access links.

priceSummary.localPrice: Open-jaw fares can be cheaper if you fly into Zurich and out of Geneva

warnings: Budget fares often exclude checked bags and seat selection.
quotes: Zurich Airport is the simplest rail-linked arrival point for central Switzerland.

provenance.label: SWISS
provenance.note: Used for flight browsing and public booking handoff.

cards.title: Hotel Interlaken
cards.summary: Central base near Interlaken Ost if you want easy train access without driving every day.
cards.trustSummary: Based on the official hotel site and Switzerland Tourism listings.

priceSummary.touristPrice: Rooms often start around CHF 240 in peak months
priceSummary.localPrice: Midweek stays outside school holidays are usually cheaper

warnings: Peak summer weekends book out early.
quotes: Best fit if you want to keep trains and mountain lifts simple.

cards.title: Boutique Hotel Glacier in Grindelwald
cards.summary: Higher-end stay if you want mountain views and quick access to Grindelwald Terminal.
cards.trustSummary: Official site details matched with tourism listings.

priceSummary.localPrice: Short shoulder-season stays can be lower

warnings: This is less convenient if you plan to spend most nights in Interlaken.
quotes: A better splurge pick than a base for constant day trips.

cards.title: Europcar pick-up near Interlaken
cards.summary: Useful if you want flexibility for valley stops, but trains are usually easier for the classic mountain route.
cards.trustSummary: Official rental site plus local driving advice for Swiss alpine routes.

priceSummary.touristPrice: Small cars often start near CHF 95 per day
priceSummary.localPrice: Winter AWD upgrades are worth paying for in snowy weeks

warnings: Some mountain villages have limited parking and high garage fees.
quotes: Great for flexible valley drives, not always the fastest choice for peak tourist routes.

cards.title: SBB rail links to Interlaken and Lauterbrunnen
cards.summary: The most reliable backbone for a Bernese Oberland itinerary. Easy transfers and frequent service throughout the day.
cards.trustSummary: Based on official SBB rail pages and station information.

priceSummary.touristPrice: Point-to-point fares can add up quickly
priceSummary.localPrice: Saver Day Pass deals can make multi-stop days cheaper

warnings: Mountain connections can run less frequently in bad weather or shoulder season.
quotes: Use Interlaken Ost as the anchor for train-heavy trip planning.

cards.title: Harder Kulm funicular from Interlaken
cards.summary: Short scenic lift for an easy viewpoint if you want a mountain moment without a full-day excursion.

priceSummary.touristPrice: Round trips are often around CHF 38
priceSummary.localPrice: Regional passes can lower the effective cost

warnings: The last descent time matters. Do not plan this too late in the day.
quotes: One of the easiest scenic add-ons if the weather is clear.

cards.title: Quiet cafe stop in Iseltwald
cards.summary: A calmer lakeside stop than the busiest Interlaken cafes if you want a slower afternoon.
cards.trustSummary: Suggested from local-style travel discussions and guide pages, then checked for location and opening details.

priceSummary.touristPrice: Coffee and cake usually land in the CHF 12-18 range
priceSummary.localPrice: Village bakeries are often cheaper than lakefront tourist terraces

warnings: Some smaller spots close early outside summer.
quotes: Good reset stop if Interlaken feels too busy.

cards.title: Traditional lunch stop in Lauterbrunnen
cards.summary: Simple Swiss lunch option that fits well between train legs and valley walks.
cards.trustSummary: Cross-checked guide listings with mapped opening times.

priceSummary.touristPrice: Lunch mains often start around CHF 24
priceSummary.localPrice: Set lunch specials can be noticeably cheaper

warnings: Kitchen hours can end earlier than the full closing time.
quotes: A practical food stop rather than a splurge restaurant.

cards.title: Mountain weather changes fast
cards.summary: Keep the highest-altitude stop flexible because wind and low cloud can cancel the payoff even when valley weather looks fine.
cards.trustSummary: Repeated across local travel discussions and official mountain transport pages.
warnings: Book the clearest weather window for mountain viewpoints, not the other way around.
quotes: Clear morning conditions often beat a late afternoon gamble.

cards.title: Expect Sunday closures in smaller towns
cards.summary: Village shops and some smaller cafes can close on Sundays, so keep snacks and backup food plans in mind.
cards.trustSummary: Common local planning tip for Switzerland day trips.
warnings: Do not assume every village stop has a late lunch option.
quotes: Sundays feel quieter in smaller Swiss towns, especially outside peak season.
